question: Design a C++ class named `Book` with private member variables for `title` (string), `author` (string), and `isbn` (string). Provide public getter and setter methods for each attribute.

test_cases.expected:
Initial Book Details:
Title: Dune
Author: Frank Herbert
ISBN: 978-0441172719

Updated Book Details (after direct modification via getter):
Title: Dune Messiah
Author: Brian Herbert & Kevin J. Anderson
ISBN: 978-0765307301

errors: The `getTitle()` method returns a `std::string&` (a non-const reference) instead of `const std::string&` or `std::string`. This breaks encapsulation by allowing external code to directly modify the private `title` member variable without using a designated setter method. Getters should typically provide read-only access or a copy of the private data.
